captured_from: Public Ollama API docs (/api/chat) — synthetic anonymised examples mirroring documented shape; WAL sampling deferred (see Phase 2 checkpoint notes).

examples.notes: Plain non-streaming Ollama /api/chat response. Ollama returns FOUR distinct timing_value fields (total/load/prompt_eval/eval durations, all nanoseconds). Ollama does NOT return cached_tokens or total_tokens; downstream code computes total. `done` boolean is a streaming-completion flag, not a finish reason — `done_reason` is the canonical finish_reason. `created_at` is wall-clock response time → response_timestamp (ISO-8601 string variant, distinct from timing_value durations).
Streaming chunk shape (intermediate, done=false). No usage / timing fields until the final chunk. message.content is the incremental token text — content.

Tool-calls response. Ollama tool_calls do NOT carry id or type — only function.name + function.arguments. `arguments` is a structured dict (not a JSON string like OpenAI). The flattener will descend into the dict; each leaf under `arguments.*` is labelled tool_call_arguments. This is a documented divergence from OpenAI — flag for the architect: do we want a single `tool_call_arguments` label on the parent or per-leaf? Current choice: per-leaf, since the encoder needs a label per FlatField.
